Bits, Bytes and Barks

January 1997
Investigative MethodsCrime & JusticeJournalism & Newsroom

A collection of short items including a story about an Indiana court clerk's refusal to release public records and multiple job listings for journalists with CAR skills.

After our issue on Freedom of Information battles, Joe Carroll of the Hammond (Ind.) Times shared this story: The criminal court clerk in a nearby city refused to let me look at a court file for a woman jailed on battery and resisting arrest charges. The court clerk said she is, by law, the Keeper of Records, not the Distributor of Records. Eventually, it took a brief lawsuit to straighten them out.

Jobs: The Miami (Fla.) Herald needs a capital bureau reporter skilled in CAR. The San Jose (Calif.) Mercury News needs technology reporters. The Orange County (Calif.) Register needs a technology reporter and an assistant city editor. The Wilmington (N.C.) Morning Star needs a court reporter.
